steps.text: Select method (Quantitative, Qualitative, Culture, Molecular, or Efficacy Test Methods)
Optimize method (Sample Collection, Sample Processing, Quality Assurance and Quality Control)
Perform Data Analysis
Perform Method Validation (Tier 1, Tier 2, or Tier 3)
Describe Method Performance Attributes
Write Final Method
Obtain Final Method Approval (Rulemaking, Alternate Method, Alternative Test Procedures, or Expedited Method Approval)

constraints.text: Although nine laboratories would be ideal for Tier 3 validation, a minimum of six laboratories is acceptable if nine is not reasonable.

steps.text: Test samples representative of the intended use of the method to determine the range and/or proportion of these samples compared to those obtained from a specified reference matrix.
Perform studies to determine whether QC materials' recoveries and responsiveness to interferences is representative of the true analyte.
Ensure that control material does not interfere with the detection of the true analyte at low concentration.

preconditions.text: QC materials are utilized in a method

steps.text: Ensure that control material does not interfere with the detection of the true analyte at low concentration.
Prepare a complete written description of the method.
Include the numerical and descriptive specifications of the method’s operational limits and performance attributes determined from the within-laboratory testing results during primary validation.

constraints.text: The described format (including scope, summary, definitions, etc.) is a recommended format and not a requirement.

steps.text: Document the study design in a study plan that provides a detailed description of the study’s objectives and the clear statement of a testable hypothesis(es).
Write any study-specific procedural instructions not fully explained in the written method in detail and provide them with the method to the laboratories well in advance of the study.
Disseminate and make results from the method performance study available to the scientific community for peer review.

preconditions.text: The study design has been determined.
Prior to presentation at a scientific meeting, publication in a peer-reviewed journal, and/or publication of a technical manual.

constraints.text: Use blind replicate samples to eliminate the potential for any selectivity by the laboratories in their procedures and reporting of test results.

steps.text: Prepare the diluted test chemical from a reagent-grade product per the preparation sheets provided by the Study Director (for sodium hypochlorite treatments).
Use HACH Test Kits to verify available chlorine for diluted samples.
Initiate a new stock culture.
Produce spores for use in the TSM per the method.

preconditions.text: An analysis of formulation chemistry must be performed on each lot by OPP chemists to confirm the percent active ingredient in advance of testing.
If using an existing stock culture, ensure it meets quality control standards (proper documentation, confirmation testing) and obtain Study Director approval.

constraints.text: No organic burden will be added to the spore inoculum.

steps.text: Review and comment on the data and supporting information.
Submit the data and supporting information to the statistician.
Communicate critical findings immediately to the Study Director.
Reject data.

preconditions.text: Microbial contamination occurs at a level unacceptable to the Study Director.

steps.text: Test for inhibitory residues on glassware periodically.

constraints.text: Personal protective clothing or devices are recommended during the handling of these items for purpose of activation, dilution, or efficacy testing.
A chemical fume hood or other containment equipment may be employed when appropriate during performing tasks with concentrated products.
The study analyst may wish to consult the Material Safety Data Sheet for the specific product/active ingredient to determine best course of action.

These microbiological methods are very technique sensitive and technique-oriented, thus exact adherence to the method, good laboratory practices, and quality control (QC) are required for proficiency and validity of the results.
Detergents used in washing glassware may leave residues which are bacteriostatic.

steps.text: Examine spore suspension with a phase contrast microscope or by staining to assess quality of the spores.
Examine a minimum of five fields and determine ratio of spores to vegetative cells (or sporangia).
Combine and re-aliquot spore suspension from multiple plates into tubes for uniformity.
Determine spore titer of the concentrated spore suspension by plating serial dilutions (e.g., 1.0 x 10^-6 through 1.0 x 10^-8) using pour or spread plating on TSA plates.
For pour plating, add molten TSA tempered to 45-55ºC to each plate, swirl, and allow agar to solidify.
Incubate plates for 24 ± 2 hours at 36 ± 1ºC and determine titer.
Dilute the suspension prior to carrier inoculation to achieve a target titer of 1.0 x 10^8 to 5.0 x 10^8 CFU/mL.

preconditions.text: Percentage of spores versus vegetative cells must be at least 95% before combining or proceeding to inoculation.

constraints.text: Percentage of spores versus vegetative cells should be at least 95%.

steps.text: Transfer carrier to a second tube of modified FTM.
For viability control, place one unexposed inoculated carrier in a separate tube of modified FTM.
For media sterility, use one tube of modified FTM.
Incubate all test and control tubes for 21 days at 36 ±
Calculate the log reduction (LR) value using the formulas appropriate for each laboratory test.
Conduct a one factor, random effects model ANOVA for each combination of test method and test treatment to estimate Sr, SR, and variability sources.
Calculate the mean LR along with the associated standard error and confidence interval for each chemical × efficacy level combination.
Perform a statistical trend test for each chemical treatment to determine whether the mean LR values increase significantly with increasing efficacy level.
Perform diagnostic plots and tests routinely to check whether the observations conform to the mathematical assumptions underlying the ANOVA calculations.
Create tables and figures to present the results.
Apply the P/N formula to calculate an LR value that is consistent with each AOAC test result.
Calculate an assessment of the percentage of the total spore counts for each fraction of the TSM.
Select and clearly describe the quantification approach (relative or absolute) and address uncertainties associated with the PCR method.
Estimate and propagate uncertainty throughout data analysis to account for sources of variability.

constraints.text: Uncertainty can arise within and between experiments from numerous sources such as inconsistencies in quality of reagents, pipet calibration, as well as dilution preparation and storage of standards.

steps.text: Detail metrics for report values in the method.
Establish the LOD and/or LOQ for the PCR method to generate a baseline performance level.

preconditions.text: Establish the LOD and/or LOQ for a PCR method to generate a baseline performance level before comparing performance in uncharacterized matrices.

constraints.text: Careful preparation and handling of standards for accuracy measurements is critical for the estimation of PCR method accuracy.

steps.text: Perform SLOD experiments in the same manner as LOD experiments (Section 2.4.4), spiking reference standards into the environmental sample matrix rather than laboratory grade water or buffer.
Designate alternative sources of reagents.

preconditions.text: Products must be experimentally demonstrated to provide equivalent performance in the method to that of the reagents from the originally specified source.
